provisions.content: -تخزينالكلنكر فيصوامع أوساحاتتخزينيةمغلقة تمامامزودةبمرشحات جافةلقمع الغبار(big filter).حيث يحظرتخزينالكلنكر فيالساحاتالمفتوحة،إلا فيالحالاتالاستثنائيةوليس لأكثر من10أيام من خلالالتغطيةالمناسبةبجدران مضادةللمياه،وإبلاغالسلطةالمختصةوالحصول علىإذن خاص.

provisions.title: يجبتخزين جميعأنواع الوقودالسائل فيصهاريج تخزينمناسبة طبقاللمعاييرالمعمول بهافي ا
provisions.content: يجبتخزين جميعأنواع الوقودالسائل فيصهاريج تخزينمناسبة طبقاللمعاييرالمعمول بهافي الدولة،بينما يتمتخزين الوقودالصلب كالفحمفي مخازنمغطاة بطريقةمناسبة،وضرورةاستخدام نظامكبح الغباربالمياهلتقليلالانبعاثات،على أن يتمتجهيز معداتمناولة موادالفحم بنظاممناسب للتحكمفي انبعاثاتالغبار(المزيد منالتفاصيل فيالملحق(1)).

provisions.content: تلتزمالمنشآتبزراعة أشجاركافية لتغطيةما لا يقل عن50٪من الحدودالخارجيةوتشكيل حزامأخضر حولالمصنع،وكذلك غرسأشجارونباتاتداخله لتحسينالبيئةوالمناظرالطبيعية.وعلى ان تكونمن الأشجارالتي تتطلبعناية وكمياتقليلة منالمياه كشجرالغاف. كما يجبالاهتمامبنظافةالمناطق داخلوحول المصنعوالمحافظةعليها.

provisions.content: 2-10التخلص منغبار الفرنالإسمنتيبإعادةتدويره فيعملية تصنيعالاسمنتومزجه معالمواد الخامالمصنعةللإسمنت(الكلنكر). بعضالحالاتالخاصة تتطلبردم الغبار؛لتقليلالانبعاثاتومنع تكوينمكوناتكيميائيةمعينة من خليطالمواد الخاموالوقود،وذلك في مكبالنفايات أوفي منطقةمحددة من قبلالسلطةالمختصة،وتلتزم مصانعالإسمنت بمنعتصاعدانبعاثاتالغبار عنطريق ضغطالغبار ورشالمياهبكمياتكافية؛ نظرالاحتوائه علىمكوناتكيميائية قدتكون ضارةبالصحة.

provisions.content: 1-12تلتزم كافةالمنشآتبتثبيتمرشحات حديثة(Bag-housefilters)لجمع وتقليلانبعاثاتالغبار، كناتلتزم بتثبيتمرشحاتإضافية (Bag-housefilters)بنسبة لا تقلعن25٪من السعةالمقدرة لتلكالمرشحات؛لتحويلالغبارالكثيفالمتصاعدوتداركاحتمالاتالانسدادوالتسرب فيالمرشحاتالأخرى.

provisions.title: 2-12يحظر علىالمنشآتإطلاق غبارأفرانالاسمنت فيالهواء، ويجبألا يتجاوزمستوىانبعاثاتغ
provisions.content: 2-12يحظر علىالمنشآتإطلاق غبارأفرانالاسمنت فيالهواء، ويجبألا يتجاوزمستوىانبعاثاتغبار الاسمنتمن المداخن عن40ملجم/مترمكعب.

provisions.title: APPENDIX-2CONTROLLING AIR POLLUTION DURING CEMENT PLANTOPERATIONS (REFERENCEDOCU
provisions.content: APPENDIX-2CONTROLLING AIR POLLUTION DURING CEMENT PLANTOPERATIONS (REFERENCEDOCUMENT ONLY)

provisions.title: The mainconstituents of exhaust gasses from cement kilns are nitrogen, carbon di
provisions.content: The mainconstituents of exhaust gasses from cement kilns are nitrogen, carbon dioxide,oxygen and moisture. The exhaust gasses also contain small quantities of dust,chlorides, fluorides, sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide and still smallerquantities of organic compounds and heavy metals. Many of these constituents areharmful to health and environment regulators are getting stricter in reducingthe emission limits. Measures should be taken to minimize the pollutantsby:

provisions.title: -Optimizing the process control by computerized automation.
provisions.content: -Optimizing the process control by computerized automation.

provisions.title: -Maximizing the limit of pre-heating and pre-calcination stages.
provisions.content: -Maximizing the limit of pre-heating and pre-calcination stages.

provisions.title: - Use ofmodern clinker coolers for maximum heat recovery.
provisions.content: - Use ofmodern clinker coolers for maximum heat recovery.

provisions.title: -Minimizing use of electrical energy with good power management system-especiall
provisions.content: -Minimizing use of electrical energy with good power management system-especially for grinding equipment.

provisions.title: - Carefulselection of raw materials & fuel to control substances entering the ki
provisions.content: - Carefulselection of raw materials & fuel to control substances entering the kiln tominimize sulfur, nitrogen, chlorides, dioxins, metals and volatile organiccompounds.

provisions.title: Allcement plants should have modern monitoring equipment for measuring the stack
provisions.content: Allcement plants should have modern monitoring equipment for measuring the stackemissions continuously for particulates,SO2 &NO2. The following limits have beenrecommended:

provisions.title: The abovelimits have been recommended with reference to the data available from
provisions.content: The abovelimits have been recommended with reference to the data available from thecurrent stack emission values of UAE cement plants.

provisions.title: NOx formsby the reaction of nitrogen with oxygen at the high temperatures genera
provisions.content: NOx formsby the reaction of nitrogen with oxygen at the high temperatures generatedduring the combustion of fuel. Emissions ofNOx are of concern as they candetrimentally affect air quality and human health, an example of which is itsrole in the production of ground-level ozone, which can aggravate respiratorysystems. According to EIPPC (2001), the best available techniques for reducingNOx emissions are a combination of the general primary measures togetherwith

provisions.title: Achievableemission levels are in the range of 200-500mg/m3 (but individual kilns
provisions.content: Achievableemission levels are in the range of 200-500mg/m3 (but individual kilns mayhave less scope to achieve these levels) according to EIPPC (2001); or 600mg/m3 (0.5 kg per ton of clinker)according to the World Bank Group (1998). Recent German cement industry datashow average emissions of approximately400-800mg/m3(VDZ, 2002).

provisions.title: Emissionsof oxides of sulfur are predominantly (99%) in the form of sulfur dioxi
provisions.content: Emissionsof oxides of sulfur are predominantly (99%) in the form of sulfur dioxide(SO2).SO2 emissions arise from oxidation ofvolatile sulfur present in raw materials -such as organic sulfur, or inorganicsulfides. Some may also arise from sulfur in the fuels. If the volatile sulfurcontent of the fuel and raw materials is low,SO2 emission can be very low. Much ofthe SO2 coproduced can potentially becaptured within the process due to strongly alkaline conditions.

provisions.title: Emissionsof SOx are of concern as they can detrimentally affect air quality and
provisions.content: Emissionsof SOx are of concern as they can detrimentally affect air quality and humanhealth, some examples of which are the production of acid rain, reducedatmospheric visibility (smog) and aggravation of respiratory systems.

provisions.title: A primarymeasure to avoid SO2 emissions is tominimize the volatile sulfur conten
provisions.content: A primarymeasure to avoid SO2 emissions is tominimize the volatile sulfur content of the raw materials. Concentrations ofSO2 in the kiln exhaust gases may bereduced by enhancing capture within the process itself. The captured sulfur isthus incorporated into the final cement product. Capture may be enhancedby:

provisions.title: -Improving the sulfur/alkali ratio by decreasing the sulfur feed or increasingth
provisions.content: -Improving the sulfur/alkali ratio by decreasing the sulfur feed or increasingthe alkali feed to the process.

provisions.title: -Increasing oxygen concentration in key zones of the process.
provisions.content: -Increasing oxygen concentration in key zones of the process.

provisions.title: -Increasing the fineness of raw materials and solid fuels.
provisions.content: -Increasing the fineness of raw materials and solid fuels.

provisions.title: -Avoiding reducing conditions (lack of oxygen) at the kiln wall.
provisions.content: -Avoiding reducing conditions (lack of oxygen) at the kiln wall.

provisions.title: -Scrubbers may be used to remove SO2from exhaust gases.
provisions.content: -Scrubbers may be used to remove SO2from exhaust gases.

provisions.title: Accordingto EIPPC (2001), emission levels of 200-400mg/m3 are achievable using b
provisions.content: Accordingto EIPPC (2001), emission levels of 200-400mg/m3 are achievable using bestavailable technologies. The World Bank Group (1998) recommends a maximumemission level of 400 mg/m3. Germanindustry data for 2001 fall well within this range(VDZ, 2002).

provisions.title: Majorsource of emission of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide is from cement producti
provisions.content: Majorsource of emission of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide is from cement production.50% of CO2 is generated during theprocess reaction in the kiln when limestone(CaCO3) converts to calciumoxide(CaO). About 40% of the CO2emission is from fossil fuel combustion in the cement kiln. The combustionconditions in the cement kiln are optimized for fuel economy.

provisions.title: Traces ofmetals are present in the cement raw materials and fuel. The release of
provisions.content: Traces ofmetals are present in the cement raw materials and fuel. The release of metalsinto the air depends on the volatility of the metal. Non-volatile metals remainin the kiln and forms part of the clinker. Dust from cement production maycontain traces of metals like arsenic, copper, cadmium, mercury, lead, nickel,zinc, etc.

provisions.title: Recentstudies by US EPA has shown emission of mercury (Hg) from cement productio
provisions.content: Recentstudies by US EPA has shown emission of mercury (Hg) from cement production, canbe a health hazard as it is the most volatile metal. Also it is suspected thatthe deposition of mercury into the sea from the cement plants may rise themercury content in the marine life, especially in the fishes for humanconsumption. US EPA has taken action to limit the release of mercury content toless than 1 ppm in the particulate matter released from cement production.

provisions.title: Cementkilns, in common with other combustion processes, are potential sources of
provisions.content: Cementkilns, in common with other combustion processes, are potential sources ofemissions of a number of semi-volatile organic substances which are of concernbecause of their highly toxic properties:

provisions.title: -Polychlorinated dibenzodioxins and polychlorinated dibenzofurans - collectively
provisions.content: -Polychlorinated dibenzodioxins and polychlorinated dibenzofurans - collectivelyknown as "dioxins".

provisions.title: Dioxinsand PCBs may be formed within the kiln if chlorine is introduced into the
provisions.content: Dioxinsand PCBs may be formed within the kiln if chlorine is introduced into the kilnas a constituent of the raw materials or fuels.. Formation of dioxins occurs atrelatively low temperatures -most typically in exhaust gases from combustionprocesses as the gases cool through a temperature window of4500C to2000C. To minimize the possibilityof dioxin formation it is important that the kiln gases are cooled through thewindow of 4500C to2000C as quickly as possible(EIPPC, 2001).

provisions.title: Dioxinemissions are of particular concern, where alternative fuels are burned. I
provisions.content: Dioxinemissions are of particular concern, where alternative fuels are burned. It hasbeen generally accepted that release of dioxins can be significantly reduced ifburned under the following conditions ( Koogbeumker, ICR, 5/1994):

provisions.title: Cementkilns can easily meet these requirements with adequate process control.
provisions.content: Cementkilns can easily meet these requirements with adequate process control.

provisions.title: 6.0Recommended Emission Standards for UAE Cement Plants
provisions.content: 6.0Recommended Emission Standards for UAE Cement Plants

provisions.title: Majorityof the cement plants constructed in the UAE are designed & constructed b
provisions.content: Majorityof the cement plants constructed in the UAE are designed & constructed bymajor international cement plant manufacturers. The selection and design of thecement plants have been made on the basis of the emission standards set by theenvironmental authorities in the advanced countries. The emission standards setby the environmental authorities of US EPA, EIPPC, World Bank and United Nationsare based on large number data and observations with respect to negativeenvironmental impacts on human health and other factors. Based on these studiesand values set in the UAE federal environmental law of 1999, it is recommendedto follow the following emission standards for major pollutants from the UAEcement plants:

provisions.title: EuropeanIntegrated Pollution Prevention Control Bureau, Serville
provisions.content: EuropeanIntegrated Pollution Prevention Control Bureau, Serville

provisions.title: Nodular hardsubstance produced from cement kiln
provisions.content: Nodular hardsubstance produced from cement kiln

provisions.title: Informal termfor family of polychlorinated dibenzo dioxins & furans
provisions.content: Informal termfor family of polychlorinated dibenzo dioxins & furans

provisions.title: A naturalmineral consisting hydrated calcium sulphate
provisions.content: A naturalmineral consisting hydrated calcium sulphate

provisions.title: A naturalrock consisting mainly calcium carbonate
provisions.content: A naturalrock consisting mainly calcium carbonate

provisions.title: Oxides ofNitrogen: Nitric Oxide (NO) plus Nitrogen Dioxide(NO2)
provisions.content: Oxides ofNitrogen: Nitric Oxide (NO) plus Nitrogen Dioxide(NO2)

provisions.title: Oxides ofSulfur: Sulfur dioxide (SO2) plusSulfur trioxide (SO3)
provisions.content: Oxides ofSulfur: Sulfur dioxide (SO2) plusSulfur trioxide (SO3)
